english: W. to suffice, *mi gáb-će med* (mi gab ce med) the workmen will not suffice:

pf. gams (gams) Sch., bgams (bgams) Cs., fut. bgam (bgam), imp. goms (goms) 1. to put, or rather throw, into the mouth, e.g. grains of wheat, a mouthful of meal, as Tibetans use to do; pye púr-mgo re tsam _gams (phye phur mgo re tsam 'gams) I took a small spoonful of meal Mil. — 2. to try, bgám-mo (bgam mo) I will try him, I shall put him to the proof Dzl.; tsód _gám-pa (tshod 'gam pa) id. Lex. — 3. W. to threaten, to menace.

(_ga-bo ('ga' bo) ('s.?)) some, a few, several, Kyí-ra-ba _ga (khyi ra ba 'ga') some huntsmen Mil.; gžón-pa _ga žig (gzhon pa 'ga' zhig) some young men Mil.; ko-ráṅ _ga (kho rang 'ga') some of them Mil.; _ga-ré ('ga' re) = _ga žig ('ga' zhig) Lth.; gál-te nán-gyis _ga žig bžág-na (gal te nan gyis 'ga' zhig bzhag na) if I appoint some by a peremptory decree Dzl.; skabs _gar (skabs 'gar) in some cases; lan _ga (žig) (lan 'ga' (zhig)) sometimes, now and then (opp. to frequently, as well as to once, one time); res _ga (res 'ga') 1. sometimes. 2. col. for some, several; bar _ga (bar 'ga') sometimes; lan _ga (lan 'ga') — lan _ga (lan 'ga'), res _ga (res 'ga') — res _ga (res 'ga'), bar _ga (bar 'ga') — bar-_ga (bar 'ga') at one time — at another time, some — others; _ga tsam ('ga' tsam) a few, few Thgy.: _ga šás ('ga' shas) some, part (of them) Mil.; _ga yáṅ ('ga' yang) followed by a negation: no, no one, not any, none.
Glr., also _ga-ti ('ga' ti) n. of a place in the east of Tibet.

1. sbst. (W. also *gár-ru* (gar ru), Ts. *gar* (gar), *gir* (gir)) masc. _gár-po ('gar po), fem. _gár-mo ('gar mo), a mixed breed of cattle, of a mdzo (mdzo) (q.v.) and a common cow, or a bull and a mdzó-mo (mdzo mo). — 2. vb. v. sub dgár-ba (dgar ba).

c. las (las) or daṅ (dang), to be in opposition or contradiction to, as: rtág-pa daṅ dṅós-po gnyis _gál-ba yin (rtag pa dang dngos po gnyis 'gal ba yin) the ideas of 'perpetuity' and of 'thing' are contradictory; commonly of persons: to counteract, to act in opposition to, to transgress, violate, infringe, break, a promise, law, duty; yid daṅ mi _gál-bar (yid dang mi 'gal bar) Dzl., resp. tugs daṅ mi _gál-bar (thugs dang mi 'gal bar), (he gives them) to their wish, to their heart's content; bka bžín-du mi _gal-bar bgyío (bka' bzhin du mi 'gal bar bgyio) I shall act faithfully according to the order Dzl.; *gal mi dug* (gal mi dug) W. he has not committed anything, he is innocent; lha (lha) or klu daṅ _gal-ba (klu dang 'gal ba) not to honour a Lha or Lu according to duty. Comp. _gal-rkyén ('gal rkyen) mishap, untoward accident, impediment (opp. to mtun-rkyén (mthun rkyen)); _gal-rkyen sél-ba ('gal rkyen sel ba), or méd-par byéd-pa (med par byed pa), or zlóg-pa (zlog pa) to avert, to remove such accidents or impediments. — _gal-_krúl ('gal 'khrul) transgression, _gal-_krúl spaṅs-te ('gal 'khrul spangs te), conscientiously; *_gal-kṛúl só-wa* ('gal 'khrul gso ba) to make amends, to atone for a transgression. — _gal-mtún-šés-pa ('gal mthun shes pa) Chr. Prot., the knowledge of what is conformable or contrary to the divine law, meant to express our 'conscience'; the term was formed after the Tibetan phrase: dge mi-dge šés-pa (dge mi dge shes pa), or rig-pa (rig pa), knowledge of what is virtue and what is vice; cf. however šés-bžin (shes bzhin), ṅón-ba (ngon ba), and byas-čós (byas chos). — _gál-ba-po ('gal ba po) Cs., _gál-po ('gal po) Sch., a transgressor. — _gal-tsábs ('gal tshabs) Cs. a great fault, a crime: _gal-tsábs-čan ('gal tshabs can) faulty, criminal, a criminal (?).

pf. gas (gas) (cf. _ges-pa ('ges pa)) to be cleft or split, of rocks etc.; to chap, of the skin, the lips; to break open, to burst, of a bag etc., lṅa lṅá-ru (lnga lnga ru) into five rents, in five places; to crack, to break or burst asunder, of a vessel, the heart, a fruit, bdún-du (bdun du) into seven pieces; šiṅ-gi rigs-la byás-na ni _gas (shing gi rigs la byas na ni 'gas) if it be made of wood, it will split, crack Glr.
gun-stock, (spelling not certain) v. sgum-mdá (sgum mda').

pf. bgug (bgug), fut. dgug (dgug), imp. kug (khug) 1. (cf. kug (khug)) to bend, to make crooked, gnya _gugs-pa (gnya 'gugs pa) C. to bend, bow, stoop; mgo _gugs-_gugs-par sóṅ-ṅo (mgo 'gugs 'gugs par song ngo) he went off bowed down, crestfallen. — 2. to gather, to cause a gathering, rnág-tu (rnag tu) of matter, pus, to suppurate. — 3. to call, to summon, to send for, e.g. the gardener Dzl.,
